root_cause: The p99 latency page for the web service was a false alarm / already-recovered transient blip. Evidence: sreft-web was running without restarts, OOMs, or high CPU/memory; logs reported the service healthy on port 8080 with release stable-1.4.2; the load generator reported all requests to http://web:8080/orders succeeding; direct host probes to http://localhost:8085/orders returned HTTP 200 with sub-3 ms latency. The compose scenario notes also identify web as healthy and the monitor page as a brief recovered blip or misconfigured threshold.
actions_taken: Investigated container status with docker ps, resource usage with docker stats, logs for web/payments/load/operator, web container state and environment with docker inspect, and the docker-compose.yaml configuration. Ran direct latency probes against the published web endpoint, confirming successful low-latency responses. Took no service-changing remediation because web was healthy and restarting or modifying containers would have added unnecessary risk. Left unrelated payments and other containers untouched.
postmortem: A latency alert fired for web even though the service was healthy by container state, logs, load-generator traffic, and direct probes. This indicates the alert was either triggered by a short already-resolved blip or by an overly sensitive/misconfigured p99 threshold. Blameless follow-up: review the monitor threshold, evaluation window, and paging policy so transient or non-actionable p99 spikes do not page unless corroborated by sustained latency, error rate, or failed health checks.
